works.title: Modular and Transformable Denim Design for Multi-Size Adaptability
works.description: A component-based denim jacket and skirt integrating adjustable side-seam gussets that enable up to two numerical sizes of expansion. When closed, the garments maintain a fitted silhouette; when opened, the gussets increase circumference to accommodate a range of body sizes. A drawcord at the jacket waist provides additional fit regulation. The final set provides a cohesive staple garment that accommodates multiple size ranges through modular design while supporting garment longevity. Co-designed with P. Sidberry.

works.title: Fruit Loops — Hemp & Organic Cotton Interlocking Ring Garment
works.venue: Hemp Impressions · LSU Human Ecology Lobby, Baton Rouge, LA; Louisiana's Old State Capitol, Baton Rouge, LA; Corvallis Museum, Oregon State University, Corvallis, OR
works.description: A top constructed from 46 snap-separating knit fabric rings in 55% hemp / 45% organic cotton. Each ring links with another, creating the straps and bodice, with layers of interconnecting rings added to lengthen the garment. The jeans were hand-dyed with a 2-inch knit insert added at the side seam for adjustable sizing, mobility, and comfort. National juried show: 20 entries submitted, 14 selected (70% acceptance rate). Co-designed with D. Michaelson.

works.title: Weaving Sustainability through Zero-Waste Design: Reinventing Machine Jamdani Saree
works.venue: Creative Showcase 2025, Gogue Performing Arts Center, Auburn University, Auburn, AL
works.description: A zero-waste dress crafted by deconstructing a machine-made Jamdani saree from Dhaka, Bangladesh. The decorative border serves as the hemline to preserve the original essence of the textile. Intricate motifs are positioned strategically on the torso to highlight the artistry of the Jamdani weave, while kimono sleeves utilize the border pattern to minimize waste. Achieves 100% fabric use in pattern-making without compromising fit and aesthetics.
